text: Since 1951, the k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm has become one of the main data mining, recommendation systems and Internet of Things (IoT) tools. This work makes a review and evaluation of the kNN search and kNN Join methods and tools used to get efficient results of kNN algorithm in high dimensional spaces. In the light of literature survey made in this work, which includes 31 search methods and 12 join methods in high dimensional spaces with providing an implementation for all of them, we explain the underlying concepts of those methods and discuss their properties and applicable fields. They also present a list of open problems and suggestions regarding this issue in order to have a pioneer work which reviews, compares and evaluates the kNN methods existing in the literature and direct further research works.
explanation: The text has a formal, survey-style tone with some awkward phrasing that can be typical of AI-generated content; the combination of generic claims and a precise but somewhat odd sentence structure suggests a higher chance of AI authorship, though a human-written abstract with a similar style is possible.
